title: NOC 43204 – Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces | GTR Immigration
description: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces under NOC 43204 serve in operational roles within Canada’s military structure. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation includes personnel who operate and maintain military equipment, weapons systems, engineering tools, and intelligence systems in support of national defense. These professionals serve within the:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group432 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionMinor Group4320 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionNOC Code43204

duties: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces may:
Analyze and operate combat information systems
Maintain and operate weapons, vehicles, heavy equipment, and engineering tools
Test automotive and mechanical systems using diagnostic equipment
Configure and operate intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance systems
Support infantry by constructing tools and systems to overcome obstacles
Participate in defense and operational missions
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required
Completion of approximately three months of basic military training
At least six months of occupational qualification training
Specialized training in military equipment operation and maintenance
Training is provided by the Canadian Armed Forces.
salary_prospects: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces typically earn:
$25,100 – $53,500 per year,depending on rank, experience, and years of service.
Additional benefits include housing allowances, pension plans, and medical coverage.
job_outlook: Positions under NOC 43204 typically require Canadian citizenship or eligibility to serve in the Canadian Armed Forces. Foreign nationals must meet strict eligibility and security requirements before applying.
While military positions have specific citizenship requirements, GTR Immigration can:
Assess your eligibility for Canada PR pathways
Guide you toward immigration programs suited to your background
Provide full documentation and PR strategy support
If you are exploring public protection or government career pathways in Canada, contact GTR Immigration today to discuss your options.
labour_market_demand: Operations Members are recruited through federal government military programs and serve across Canada and internationally.
Opportunities for domestic and international deployment
Military operational roles remain essential to Canada’s national defense infrastructure.
